text: This is the video demonstrating laparoscopic radical prostatectomy, a procedure done for carcinoma of the prostate gland.
The surgeon dissects the bladder and prostate from the anterior abdominal wall, carefully separating these structures from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon opens the bladder neck and proceeds to dissect the prostate, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon dissects and excises the prostate gland along with the seminal vesicles, separating these structures from surrounding tissues during the laparoscopic radical prostatectomy.
Following the removal of the prostate gland and seminal vesicles, the surgeon performs an anastomosis, connecting the bladder to the distal urethra.
Following the anastomosis, the surgeon places the prostate gland into a retrieval pad and extracts it through a small incision in the anterior abdominal wall.
